description: O/U-difference spine chart: one gray Bar-from-zero + Circle-dot lollipop per member showing the signed variance of the actual combined score from the betting/reference line, newest-first, with a +/-43 reference band and the same season/id/line label columns. The companion 'diff' view to the ww-ou-arrow chart, isolated from the Super Bowl 'O/U Line' golden worksheet.
avoid_when: the actual field is not a compound '<prefix> <winner>-<loser>' score string — the nested SPLIT/TRIM parse assumes a single-token prefix, a space, then a hyphen-separated winner-loser pair (e.g. 'PHI 40-22')
the member label is not a '<name> (<year>)' pattern — the season/id split parses the parenthetical year out of it
you want the over/under direction encoded by hue on this view — the spine marks are intentionally gray; over/under teal/red belongs to the arrow chart

slots.notes: Compound member label '<name> (<year>)'. SPLIT-parsed by the season and id calcs into the row header text columns. Never shelved raw.
Compound-score STRING SPLIT-parsed into winning + losing scores that sum to the actual combined score feeding the O/U-diff. Never shelved raw.
The O/U betting line. Shown as a discrete text label column on rows ([sum:Total Score Bet:ok]) and used as the subtrahend of the O/U-diff calc.

calcs.notes: TRIM(SPLIT([Super Bowl],'(',1)) — member name/id before the parenthetical year; feeds the SB # roman-numeral label calc.
int(TRIM(SPLIT(SPLIT([Super Bowl],'(',2),')',1)))-1 — the season year. Outermost rows dimension and the DESC (newest-first) sort key.

calcs.formula:
"SB "+CASE int([Super Bowl - Split 1])
    WHEN 2 THEN "II"
    WHEN 3 THEN "III"
    WHEN 4 THEN "IV"
    WHEN 5 THEN "V"
    WHEN 6 THEN "VI"
    WHEN 7 THEN "VII"
    WHEN 8 THEN "VIII"
    WHEN 9 THEN "IX"
    WHEN 10 THEN "X"
    WHEN 11 THEN "XI"
    WHEN 12 THEN "XII"
    WHEN 13 THEN "XIII"
    WHEN 14 THEN "XIV"
    WHEN 15 THEN "XV"
    WHEN 16 THEN "XVI"
    WHEN 17 THEN "XVII"
    WHEN 18 THEN "XVIII"
    WHEN 19 THEN "XIX"
    WHEN 20 THEN "XX"
    WHEN 21 THEN "XXI"
    WHEN 22 THEN "XXII"
    WHEN 23 THEN "XXIII"
    WHEN 24 THEN "XXIV"
    WHEN 25 THEN "XXV"
    WHEN 26 THEN "XXVI"
    WHEN 27 THEN "XXVII"
    WHEN 28 THEN "XXVIII"
    WHEN 29 THEN "XXIX"
    WHEN 30 THEN "XXX"
    WHEN 31 THEN "XXXI"
    WHEN 32 THEN "XXXII"
    WHEN 33 THEN "XXXIII"
    WHEN 34 THEN "XXXIV"
    WHEN 35 THEN "XXXV"
    WHEN 36 THEN "XXXVI"
    WHEN 37 THEN "XXXVII"
    WHEN 38 THEN "XXXVIII"
    WHEN 39 THEN "XXXIX"
    WHEN 40 THEN "XL"
    WHEN 41 THEN "XLI"
    WHEN 42 THEN "XLII"
    WHEN 43 THEN "XLIII"
    WHEN 44 THEN "XLIV"
    WHEN 45 THEN "XLV"
    WHEN 46 THEN "XLVI"
    WHEN 47 THEN "XLVII"
    WHEN 48 THEN "XLVIII"
    WHEN 49 THEN "XLIX"
    WHEN 50 THEN "L"
    WHEN 51 THEN "LI"
    WHEN 52 THEN "LII"
    WHEN 53 THEN "LIII"
    WHEN 54 THEN "LIV"
    WHEN 55 THEN "LV"
    WHEN 56 THEN "LVI"
    WHEN 57 THEN "LVII"
    WHEN 58 THEN "LVIII"
    WHEN 59 THEN "LIX"
    
END
calcs.notes: 'SB '+CASE int([Super Bowl - Split 1]) WHEN ... — the roman-numeral member label text column (template-INTERNAL: consumes the id calc).
TRIM(SPLIT([Final],'-',1)) — the winner-side token of the compound score.
INT(SPLIT([Final - Split 1],' ',2)) — the winning team's points (template-INTERNAL).
[Final - Split 1 - Split 2]+[Final - Split 2] — the actual combined score (template-INTERNAL input to the O/U-diff calc; not shelved directly in the diff view).

sign(([Calculation_3985421010944000])-([Total Score Bet])) — RESIDUAL datasource calc (present in the golden datasource) but NOT shelved in the diff view (the spine is gray). Kept so the diff and arrow share one datasource calc set.
([Calculation_3985421010944000])-([Total Score Bet]) — the signed variance from the line. The spine axis: pane 6 Bar-from-zero + pane 7 Circle dot, with the +/-43 reference band.

hazards.detail: The golden has ONE combined worksheet; this diff-only view is a documented derivation of it (cols restricted to the O/U-diff spine, arrow panes 3/4/5 removed, +/-43 reference lines relocated into the Bar pane). It could NOT be live-render-verified in this environment (no MCP apply tools), so render_verified stays 'none'. Renderability of the isolated spine is a residual risk until a live golden-match.

The actual-score and season/id calcs parse combined tokens out of compound strings via nested SPLIT/TRIM (golden semantics). Dataset-shape-specific — a different string shape yields NULL/garbage. Advisory, not a hard fast_path_blocker.
Calcs reference bare bindable [Super Bowl]/[Final]/[Total Score Bet]; templates.ts rewriteFormulaFieldRefs repairs them on bind. Template-internal calc-to-calc refs are left unmapped.
